summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #233, #235, and #236 — four framework-agnostic input component state machines (PasswordInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, PinInput) as part of Epic #220 (Agnostic input components), landing them in a single bundled PR.
summary.outcome: All four components were implemented TDD-style with full unit tests, snapshot fixtures, proptest invariants, and spec-conformance assertions. All 10 cargo xci-fast gates passed, a post-implementation audit was run, the Duration convention fix was landed after user feedback, and the commit was staged and pushed with a PR opened closing #233, #235, and #236.

repo: `#[derive(ComponentPart)]` supports struct variants (e.g., `Input { cell_index: usize }`) — proven by existing tests in `crates/ars-derive/src/component_part.rs:531-553`.
The workspace convention for time-interval fields is `std::time::Duration` / `Option<Duration>`, not `u32` milliseconds — both Tooltip and Toast follow this pattern.
`spec_conformance/input.rs` did not exist prior to this session; a new category file must be created when adding the first component in a category.
CLAUDE.md explicitly permits resolving spec/code mismatches (e.g., duplicate `FormatOptions`) in the same task/PR.
Coverage thresholds for `ars-components` are ≥90% line / ≥90% region; enforced by `xtask::coverage::default_thresholds`.

`cargo xci --profile fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate (~5 min, 10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count).
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` is the canonical orphaned-snapshot check.
`ars_i18n::number::parse_locale_number(input, locale) -> Option<f64>` is the correct API for locale-aware number parsing in NumberInput.
PinInput callbacks must use `Fn(String)` not `Fn(&str)` because `Callback<F>` requires `Args: 'static`.
The `PendingEffect::named` / `cancel_effect` pattern for debounce timers is established in `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/tooltip.rs` and should be mirrored for any debounced component.

code.finding: Existing test proves `#[derive(ComponentPart)]` handles struct variants — PinInput's `Part::Input { cell_index: usize }` requires no changes to the derive crate.
`parse_locale_number(input, locale) -> Option<f64>` — the correct API for locale-aware parsing in NumberInput's Change handler.
`RoundingMode::HalfUp` variant location — used in `FormatOptions.rounding_mode` for NumberInput's round-half-up behavior.

Required adding a new `From<F>` impl for `MessageFn<dyn Fn(usize, usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>` to support PinInput's `Messages::ordinal_label` 3-argument callback — this was a gap in the existing impl coverage.

Canonical debounce/timer pattern: emit `Effect::SearchDebounce` via `.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(...))`, cancel via `.cancel_effect(...)`. Adapters own wall-clock timer and fire `Event::DebounceExpired`.

workflow: TDD cycle for each component: read spec §1.x/§2 → write failing tests → implement public surface → implement `Machine::transition` → accept first-run snapshots with `cargo insta review`.

Post-implementation-audit skill runs three phases in sequence: (1) spec/impl drift, (2) iterative 'anything missing?' passes, (3) test coverage audit — must run before opening PR per CLAUDE.md workflow step 7.
Bundled PRs for related components (precedent: PR #654 bundled Toggle + Swap) should use four logical commits, one per component, each independently green.
After pushing a PR that adds snapshot fixtures, immediately apply the `snapshot-reviewed` label to signal reviewers that `.snap` files are intentional.
`cargo xtask spec validate` validates all spec frontmatter; `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` checks code examples in spec — both required before push.
Coverage tooling (`cargo llvm-cov`) output is hard to filter per-file; plan extra time for coverage investigation if thresholds are a concern.

friction: API Error: socket connection closed unexpectedly during initial parallel agent exploration — required re-launching exploration sequentially.
API Error: 529 Overloaded when user first requested commit/push — required retrying the session.

PinInput compilation required multiple fix iterations: `StrongSend` API didn't exist, `MessageFn` lacked a 3-argument `From<F>` impl, `readonly` check used wrong field name — each required a separate read-compile-fix cycle.
Multiple clippy rounds needed across NumberInput and PinInput: needless borrows, redundant closures, complex type aliases, missing derives — approximately 8 separate edit passes.
Coverage tooling was difficult to query precisely — many attempts to filter the lcov report to specific new files before finding a workable approach.

Post-implementation-audit (Phase 2) missed the `debounce_ms: Option<u32>` workspace convention violation; the user had to point it out explicitly, requiring a full Duration migration pass after the audit was declared complete.
open_items: Builder method clearers (`no_debounce()`, `no_format_options()`, etc.) in SearchInput, NumberInput, and PinInput are not covered by unit tests — acknowledged as trivial setters with no logic, but a known gap.
Component catalog (`spec/foundation/02-component-catalog.md`) was checked but not updated to list the four new components — verify whether this file requires manual updates when new components land.

Adapter spec directories were checked but no adapter scaffolding was added for the four new components — adapter implementations (React, Svelte, etc.) are out of scope for this PR but will be needed downstream.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill invocation is pending — must be triggered after the PR is confirmed open on GitHub.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be re-applied after any future snapshot churn on this PR (e.g., if a Codex review triggers snapshot regeneration).
